title: sphaeralcea subhastata var. latifolia
intro: Sphaeralcea subhastata var. latifolia, also known as the Mexican globe mallow or California globe mallow, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of saponins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Sphaeralcea subhastata var. latifolia have been linked to various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and boosted immune system function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
